results.title: Singtel logs 14% rise in underlying profit on Optus, regional partners' boost - Reuters
results.content: REUTERS/Edgar Su/File Photo Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab Nov 12 (Reuters) - Singapore Telecommunications (STEL.SI), opens new tab posted a 14% rise in first-half underlying profit on Wednesday, helped by a strong performance from its Australian unit Optus and regional associates, and said it expects higher operating earnings from its core units. results.title: Singtel profit increases by 14% amid Airtel stake sale - Telecoms.com
results.content: Singtel logo reads "SingTel", to the left of it 2 red squares and a black swoosh-like symbolImage 12Service Provider Singapore headquartered operator group Singtel reported one-off gains from its Airtel stake sale, while underperformance in the home mobile market offset growth in Australia. At its strategic initiative consolidating Singtel's digital infrastructure operations, Digital InfraCo, revenue dropped 1.8%, mainly due to lower fees from project-based satellite deployment services. EBITDA grew 7.2% and operating income was up 29% partly due to lower utility expenses despite expansion costs for RE:AI, its AI Cloud service offering to enterprises. Prior to joining the team, she held a number of roles at the GSMA, the mobile industry association, leading on strategic research and analytical output on mobile as an assistive technology and mobile networks and services.

results.title: Singtel expands enterprise push with Brazil office opening - Developing Telecoms
results.content: ## Singtel expands enterprise push with Brazil office opening. Singaporean operator Singtel has opened its eighth global office in Brazil as it looks to tap fresh enterprise opportunities in Latin America’s largest economy. Brazil’s enterprise telecoms market is growing at a CAGR of 7.2%, underlining the scale of the opportunity. “Singtel’s presence in Brazil reflects our confidence in Latin America’s digital growth, the region’s accelerating adoption of next-generation technologies and its increasing importance in the global digital economy,” said Ng Tian Chong, CEO of Singtel Singapore. Singtel noted it already serves several multinational customers with operations in Brazil and plans to expand its enterprise solutions across sectors including manufacturing, logistics, financial services and retail. Angela Torres-Andresen, founder and executive director of the Latin American Chamber of Commerce, said Singtel’s move highlights Latin America as a key growth market for Asian technology firms, pointing to Brazil’s strong infrastructure and deep pool of tech-savvy talent as foundations for accelerating enterprise digital transformation.

results.title: Big-ticket deals lift Singapore M&A as volumes fall - Asian Business Review
results.content: Singapore’s mergers and acquisitions (M&A) are tilting toward bigger transactions as deal values surge even whilst volumes fall, pointing to more selective capital deployment. M&As in the first five months more than doubled to $84.5b (US$65.9b), the second-highest level on record, Vianca Sanchez, a deal intelligence analyst at London Stock Exchange Group Plc, told ***Singapore Business Review*** in an emailed reply to questions. She said eight transactions above $1.3b (US$1b) contributed $61.4b (US$47.9b), accounting for 73% of total deal value, up from 40% a year earlier. Stephen Bates, a partner and head of deal advisory at KPMG in Singapore, said quarterly volumes remained broadly stable at 70 to 80 transactions, but total value rose on a higher share of large deals. by KKR & Co., Inc. and Singapore Telecommunications Ltd. There’s also KKR’s purchase of a majority stake in XCL Education Holdings Pte. Ltd., valuing the company at about $1.7b (US$1.3b).
